_comment: W3 account dimension — CI orchestration config (read by the infra-staging / infra-prod callers and the ttl-reaper via jq). NOT a *.auto.tfvars.json, so terraform never auto-loads it. HEAD-vs-tag: this file (topology + pins) is read from HEAD; tf code + regions.auto.tfvars.json (scalars) come from the pinned ref. Per-account fields: bootstrap_complete = state bucket + CI roles exist (replaces the repo-level BOOTSTRAP_COMPLETE var for the W3 path); environment = workload overlay selector, passed to terraform as TF_VAR_environment; deployment_account_id = OPTIONAL, on exactly ONE account: that account's platform apply owns the shared-registry resources in the aegis-deployment account (deployment-ecr.tf single-owner gate; move the field to migrate ownership); operator_principal_arn = the account's SSO PlatformAdmin role for the EKS operator access entry — inherently per-account (both the account id and the AWSReservedSSO name hash differ per account, so one repo secret cannot serve two accounts; ARNs are topology, not secrets). See docs/runbooks/2026-06-12-joint-strike.md §W3 and the accounts-dimension ADR.
